name: Minnesota SNAP Benefits
description: Minnesota's SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on an EBT card through county human services offices. Benefits help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries at authorized retailers and farmers markets.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 165% FPL for most households

name: Minnesota Medical Assistance (Medicaid)
description: Minnesota's Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage to eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, behavioral health, dental, and vision services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 275% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 278% FPL

name: Minnesota Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are calculated based on previous wages and can last up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit based on previous wages; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Minnesota Housing Finance Agency
description: Provides affordable housing programs including homebuyer assistance, rental assistance, homelessness prevention, and affordable housing development financing for Minnesotans.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Minnesota
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; generally targets households below 80% of area median income

name: Legal Aid State Support (Mid-Minnesota Legal Aid)
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents across Minnesota. Handles cases involving housing, family law, public benefits, immigration, disability rights, and elder law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Minnesota residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Southern Minnesota Regional Legal Services (SMRLS)
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in southern Minnesota. Handles cases involving housing, family law, immigration, public benefits, and employment.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in southern Minnesota with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Minnesota CareerForce Centers
description: Minnesota's network of workforce development centers providing free employment services including job search, career counseling, resume writing, skills assessments, and training opportunities.
eligibilitySummary: All Minnesota job seekers and employers

name: Minnesota Energy Assistance Program (EAP/LIHEAP)
description: Provides financial assistance to low-income households to help pay heating and electric bills. Benefits are paid directly to utility companies on behalf of eligible households.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 50% of state median income
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 50% of state median income

name: Minnesota 211 / United Way 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Minnesotans to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone, text, and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Minnesota seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, Somali, Hmong, and interpreter services available

name: Second Harvest Heartland
description: The largest hunger relief organization in the Upper Midwest and a Feeding America member. Serves 59 counties in Minnesota and western Wisconsin through a network of over 1,000 partner food shelves and programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Minnesota

name: Channel One Food Bank (Rochester)
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 14 counties in southeastern Minnesota. Distributes food through partner agencies and mobile food distributions.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in southeastern Minnesota

name: Minnesota Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides services to Minnesota veterans and their families including benefits counseling, claims assistance, employment programs, state veterans' homes, and the Veterans Linkage Line.
eligibilitySummary: Minnesota veterans and their dependents

name: Minnesota Crisis Text Line and Mental Health Crisis
description: Provides crisis intervention services through county-based crisis teams. Minnesotans can also text MN to 741741 for the Crisis Text Line. Free and confidential support 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Minnesota experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, Somali, Hmong, and interpreter services available

name: Minnesota Child Care Assistance Program (CCAP)
description: Provides child care subsidies to eligible families to help pay for child care while parents work, attend school, or participate in approved activities. Administered through county human services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who are working or in approved activities
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 67% of state median income at entry; up to 85% SMI to continue

name: Minnesota Family Investment Program (MFIP)
description: Minnesota's TANF program providing cash and food assistance to low-income families with children. Includes employment and training services to help families achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; very low income required

name: Minnesota WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: MinnesotaCare
description: Minnesota's state-sponsored health insurance program for people who earn too much for Medical Assistance but cannot afford private health insurance. Provides comprehensive health coverage with small premiums.
eligibilitySummary: Adults and children with income between 138-200% FPL who do not have access to employer insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Income between 138% and 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Metro Transit - Twin Cities
description: Operates bus and light rail service in the Minneapolis-St. Paul metropolitan area. Offers reduced fare programs and the Transit Assistance Program (TAP) for low-income riders.
eligibilitySummary: Twin Cities residents; TAP reduced fares for riders at or below 185% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Transit Assistance Program for households at or below 185% FPL
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 7:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Hennepin County Human Services (Minneapolis)
description: Provides comprehensive social services to Hennepin County residents including economic assistance, housing support, behavioral health, child protection, and adult services.
eligibilitySummary: Hennepin County residents in need of social services

name: Ramsey County Financial Assistance (St. Paul)
description: Provides financial assistance and social services to Ramsey County residents including SNAP, cash assistance, emergency assistance, housing support, and Medicaid enrollment.
eligibilitySummary: Ramsey County residents in need of financial assistance and social services

name: Minnesota Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating system repairs to reduce energy costs and improve comfort.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Minnesota Vocational Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Includes career counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Minnesota State Grant Program
description: Minnesota's largest state financial aid program providing need-based grants to Minnesota residents attending eligible postsecondary institutions. Awards do not require repayment.
eligibilitySummary: Minnesota residents attending eligible institutions with demonstrated financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Must demonstrate financial need as determined by FAFSA

name: Minnesota Adult Basic Education
description: Provides free classes for adults including GED preparation, English language learning, basic literacy, citizenship preparation, and workforce readiness at locations across Minnesota.
eligibilitySummary: Adults without a high school diploma or needing English language skills

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Minnesota
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Day One Minnesota - Domestic Violence Crisis Line
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals for survivors of domestic violence. Connects callers with local shelters, legal advocacy, and support services across Minnesota.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, Somali, and interpreter services available

name: Minneapolis Public Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) to low-income families in Minneapolis. Manages public housing communities and rental assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Minneapolis
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 50% of area median income for Section 8

name: Minnesota Emergency Assistance
description: Provides emergency financial assistance to families with children facing a housing emergency or loss of essential resources. Helps prevent homelessness and family crisis.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children facing an emergency threat to housing or loss of essential resources
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Mid-Minnesota Legal Aid - Senior Law Project
description: Provides free legal services to seniors 60+ in Minnesota on issues including housing, benefits, health care, consumer fraud, and elder abuse. No income requirement for seniors.
eligibilitySummary: Minnesota residents age 60 and older regardless of income

name: Duluth Transit Authority
description: Operates bus service in the Duluth area with reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders. STRIDE provides paratransit service.
eligibilitySummary: Duluth area residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and Medicare holders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM

name: Minnesota Disability Law Center
description: Provides free legal advocacy for people with disabilities in Minnesota. Handles issues including discrimination, access to services, abuse and neglect, education, employment, and housing rights.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Minnesota

name: Minnesota Immigrant and Refugee Services
description: Provides support services for immigrants and refugees in Minnesota including employment assistance, English language classes, cultural orientation, case management, and benefits navigation.
eligibilitySummary: Refugees and certain immigrants in Minnesota
languages: English, Spanish, Somali, Hmong, Karen, and other languages
